records.title: Non-residents Records Database registration (RNI)
records.description: People who will stay in the Netherlands for less than four months, or who live abroad but have dealings with Dutch authorities, can register as a non-resident in the Personal Records Database. Registration is handled by an RNI municipality and results in a citizen service number if one has not already been assigned.

official_source.title: When should I register with the Personal Records Database as a resident?

records.ai_summary: Use RNI registration when the stay in the Netherlands is shorter than four months or when a person living abroad needs a Dutch government registration. Longer stays normally require resident registration with the municipality.

records.title: Report a change of address
records.description: A move must be reported to the municipality at the new address. It may be reported from four weeks before the move until five days afterwards, and the municipality records the new address in the Personal Records Database.

official_source.title: Hoe kan ik mijn verhuizing doorgeven aan de gemeente?

records.ai_summary: Report the move to the municipality where the new home is located and provide valid identification plus evidence of occupancy. Reporting on time helps keep the BRP address correct for government services and correspondence.

attributes.reporting_window: From four weeks before until five days after the move

records.title: Certificate of Conduct (VOG)
records.description: A Certificate of Conduct states that a person's judicial history does not prevent them from carrying out a specific job, task or purpose. Justis assesses the application against the relevant screening profile.
records.ai_summary: A VOG is requested for a defined role or purpose, often after an employer or organisation starts the application. Justis checks relevant judicial information and issues the certificate when no objection exists for that purpose.

records.title: Apply for or renew a Dutch driving licence
records.description: Applications and renewals for a Dutch driving licence are normally handled by the municipality where the applicant is registered. The applicable requirements depend on the licence category and the applicant's circumstances.

official_source.title: Applying for or renewing a driving licence

records.ai_summary: Use the municipality procedure to apply for a first Dutch driving licence or renew an existing one. Check the official requirements for identification, photographs, collection and any medical assessment that applies.

records.title: Child benefit (kinderbijslag)
records.description: Child benefit is a payment from the Social Insurance Bank towards the costs of raising eligible children. The official service explains eligibility, application steps, payment dates and changes that must be reported.
records.ai_summary: The SVB administers child benefit for eligible children. After a birth or another change in family circumstances, use the official service to check whether an application is needed and keep relevant details current.

records.title: Apply for unemployment benefit (WW)
records.description: Eligible employees who become unemployed can apply to UWV for unemployment benefit through Mijn UWV. The application window opens one week before the first day of unemployment and normally closes one week after that date.
records.ai_summary: Apply through Mijn UWV close to the first unemployment date and have DigiD or a recognised European login ready. Employment details, a recent payslip, the employment contract and a bank account may be needed.

records.title: AOW state pension
records.description: AOW is the Dutch basic state pension for people who reached the applicable AOW pension age. Entitlement is built through insured years, usually by living or working in the Netherlands, and the amount also depends on the person's living situation.
records.ai_summary: AOW provides a basic state pension from the applicable pension age. People living in the Netherlands are normally contacted before that age, while periods lived or worked abroad can affect entitlement and the final amount.

records.title: Apply for student finance
records.description: Students in eligible secondary vocational or higher education programmes can apply to DUO for student finance. Available components can include a basic grant, supplementary grant, student travel product and loan, depending on the programme and personal eligibility.
records.ai_summary: Apply through Mijn DUO, preferably before the programme starts, and request the supplementary grant as part of the application so DUO can assess it. Students without digital access can use the official forms.

records.title: Register a birth
records.description: A child born in the Netherlands must be registered with the municipality where the birth took place within three days. The municipality records the birth and issues a birth certificate as the official proof of registration.
records.ai_summary: Register the child at the municipality of birth within the official three-day period. The person making the declaration should check who is authorised to register the birth and which identification or supporting documents are required.

attributes.responsible_authority: Municipality where the child was born

records.title: Notify a municipality of marriage or registered partnership
records.description: Couples planning to marry or enter into a registered partnership notify the municipality where the ceremony will take place. The municipality checks the arrangements, required documents, witnesses and any local procedure or fees.

official_source.title: What do I need to take into account if I decide to marry or enter into a civil partnership?

records.ai_summary: Contact the ceremony municipality and provide notice at least two weeks in advance under the national guidance. The couple must arrange adult witnesses and follow the municipality's process for documents, scheduling and costs.

records.title: Record a choice in the Donor Register
records.description: The Donor Register records a person's decision about organ and tissue donation after death. A choice can be recorded or changed through the official register, including online with DigiD or by using the official paper form.

official_source.title: How do I record my choice?

records.ai_summary: Use the official Donor Register to record or update the organ and tissue donation choice. Online access uses DigiD, while the official paper form provides an alternative route for recording the decision.

records.title: Child budget (kindgebonden budget)
records.description: Child budget is an income-related contribution towards the costs of children under 18. Eligibility depends on current conditions including child benefit entitlement, household income and assets, and the service is administered by Dienst Toeslagen.
records.ai_summary: Check the current income, asset and child benefit conditions for kindgebonden budget. Dienst Toeslagen may grant it automatically in some situations; otherwise the household can review and manage eligibility through Mijn Toeslagen.
